zh.Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aqu: 法國努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Aymery VIII de Rochechouart於大約1210年出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特時，他的父親Aymery VII de Rochechouart32歲，母親Alix de Mortemart11208歲。他於1233年與Marguerite de Limoges在法國努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1245年9月28日在他的家鄉享年36歲，埋葬在法國努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特。

zh-hans.Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aqu: 法国努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aymery VIII de Rochechouart于大约1210年出生在法国努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特时，他的父亲Aymery VII de Rochechouart32岁，母亲Alix de Mortemart11208岁。他于1233年与Marguerite de Limoges在法国努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1245年9月28日在他的家乡享年36岁，埋葬在法国努維勒-阿吉泰豪-維安洛瓦爾特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Aymery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the ancient Germanic personal name Amalric, composed of the elements Amal (a dynastic name among the Goths) + rīk ‘realm, power’. The name was introduced into England from France by the Normans, developing into the Middle English personal name Amery, Emery (from Old French Amauri, Emaurri).
occasionally perhaps also from the Middle English, Old French personal name Aimery, ancient Germanic Haimeric, from haim ‘home’+ rīc ‘power(ful)’. Compare Emery .

variants.name: Amber
meanings: From the word for the fossilized resin amber, a word derived via Old French and Latin from Arabic ambar. This was first used as a given name at the end of the 19th century. It enjoyed a surge in popularity following the publication of Kathleen Winsor's novel Forever Amber in 1944 , and again in the 1990s.

variants.name: Hembree
meanings: Altered form of English Hembrey, a variant of Amery , with excrescent H-.

variants.name: Emery
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, derived in the Middle Ages from a personal name introduced to England by the Normans. This was originally composed of the Germanic elements amal ‘bravery, vigour’ + rīc ‘power’, but soon assumed a variety of forms such as Amauri and Emauri.

nameMeanings.name: VIII
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Feminine pet form of   Vivien   and   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Aymery VIII de Rochechouart was born about 1210, in Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, his father, Aymery VII de Rochechouart, was 32 and his mother, Alix de Mortemart, was 11208. He married Marguerite de Limoges in 1233, in Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 28 September 1245, in his hometown, at the age of 36, and was buried in Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aymery VIII de Rochechouart est né environ 1210, à Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Aymery VII de Rochechouart, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Alix de Mortemart, avait 11208 ans. Il a épousé Marguerite de Limoges en 1233, à Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 28 septembre 1245, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 36 ans, et a été enterré à Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aymery VIII de Rochechouart um 1210 in Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Aymery VII de Rochechouart 32 und seine Mutter Alix de Mortemart 11208. Er hat 1233 in Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich Marguerite de Limoges geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 28. September 1245 im Alter von 36 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aymery VIII de Rochechouart è nato il circa 1210 nel Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, il suo padre, Aymery VII de Rochechouart, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Alix de Mortemart, aveva 11208 anni. Lui sposato Marguerite de Limoges nel 1233, nel Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 28 settembre 1245, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 36 anni, e fu sepolto a Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia.

ja.Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aqu: フランスノウベレ-アクイティアインハウテ-ヴィエンロチェチオウアルト
ja.lifeSummary: Aymery VIII de Rochechouartが約1210年にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインハウテ-ヴィエンロチェチオウアルトで生まれた時，父Aymery VII de Rochechouartは32歳，母Alix de Mortemartは11208歳であった。1233年にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインハウテ-ヴィエンロチェチオウアルトでMarguerite de Limogesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1245年9月28日に故郷で36歳で死去し，フランスノウベレ-アクイティアインハウテ-ヴィエンロチェチオウアルトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Aymery VIII de Rochechouart(이)가 대략 1210년 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 하테-비니 록헤초우아르트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Aymery VII de Rochechouart(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Alix de Mortemart(은)는 11208세였다. 그는 1233년 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 하테-비니 록헤초우아르트에서 Marguerite de Limoges(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1245년 9월 28일 그의 고향에서 36세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 하테-비니 록헤초우아르트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aymery VIII de Rochechouart nasceu aproximadamente 1210, em Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Aymery VII de Rochechouart, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Alix de Mortemart, tinha 11208 anos. Ele casou-se com Marguerite de Limoges em 1233, em Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 28 de setembro de 1245, em sua cidade natal, com 36 anos, e foi sepultado em Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aymery VIII de Rochechouart родился около 1210 г. в местности рошечоуарт, хауте-виенн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, его отцу по имени Aymery VII de Rochechouart было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Alix de Mortemart, 11208. Он женился на женщине по имени Marguerite de Limoges в 1233 г. в местности рошечоуарт, хауте-виенн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 28 сентября 1245 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 36 лет и был похоронен в местности рошечоуарт, хауте-виенн, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция.

sm.from 1243 to 1245: mai le 1243 e o'o I le 1245

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Aymery VIII de Rochechouart nació aproximadamente 1210, en Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Aymery VII de Rochechouart, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Alix de Mortemart, tenía 11208 años. Se casó con Marguerite de Limoges en 1233, en Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 28 de septiembre de 1245, en su pueblo, a la edad de 36 años, y fue sepultado en Rochechouart, Haute-Vienne, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia.
